articles.title: Oil Prices Dip as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Revives Iran Deal Hopes
articles.description: Oil prices fell after a U.S.-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire raised hopes for a broader regional deal, though the closed Strait of Hormuz continues to strain global supply.

articles.title: Gold rises on softer dollar, oil as markets weigh US-Iran peace optimism
articles.description: Gold prices dipped on Wednesday, pressured by a strengthening dollar and escalating oil prices amidst renewed Middle East conflict. Hopes for a quick U.S.-Iran resolution have faded, impacting diplomatic progress. Analysts suggest this geopolitical tension, coupled with inflation risks and potential interest rate hikes, is weighing on the precious metal.

articles.title: Stock market today: Nifty seen opening lower amid global uncertainty and FII selling
articles.description: Indian markets may open lower as investors track RBI policy, US-Iran tensions, crude oil prices and persistent FII outflows amid weak global cues.

articles.title: ‘India’s growth at risk’: Former IMF chief economist Gita Gopinath warns high oil prices could trim GDP to 6%
articles.description: Gita Gopinath also warned that such a global slowdown would place greater stress on India’s growth prospects, implying deeper downside risk than the roughly half-percentage-point gap between 6.5 per cent and a possible 6 percent outcome.

articles.title: Petrol, Diesel Price Today: Has India seen another hike on June 4? Check fuel rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Gurgaon, Chennai and other cities
articles.description: Petrol, Diesel Price Today: Fuel prices held steady across India on June 4, offering consumers a brief respite. However, rising global crude oil prices, nearing $95 per barrel, are fueling speculation about potential hikes. Such an increase could significantly impact household budgets, transportation costs, and overall inflation, making future fuel rates a key economic indicator to watch.

articles.title: Oil Price Today (June 4): Crude oil slips as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire fuels Iran war peace hopes. What’s next?
articles.description: Oil prices dipped as a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon sparked hopes for broader diplomatic progress, potentially easing U.S.-Iran tensions. The U.S. House also passed a resolution limiting President Trump's military action against Tehran. Despite these developments, analysts warn that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could significantly impact global supply for years.

articles.title: CNBC Daily Open: Fresh Iran war worries halt stock rally
articles.description: Oil prices rise and markets fall after Netanyahu tells CNBC Israel and U.S. prepared to attack Iran again if necessary.

articles.title: Cybersecurity Company Drags Down S&P 500
articles.description: Oil prices rose on Wednesday following the latest threats to the US-Iran ceasefire, and US stocks retreated from their records. The S&P 500's nine-day winning streak was...

articles.title: Sensex, Nifty set for cautious start - GIFT Nifty down 17 points; Brent crude at $97
articles.description: Share market today, June 04: Global markets drop amid US-Iran tension, raising crude oil prices—stay updated on key financial trends and rates today!

articles.title: Dollar clings to 2-month high as Gulf hostilities flare, yen wobbles near intervention zone
articles.description: Heightened Middle East tensions, including Iranian attacks and U.S. strikes, propelled oil prices and boosted the dollar's safe-haven appeal near a two-month peak. The yen teetered around the 160 mark, sparking intervention fears. Meanwhile, strong U.S. services inflation data reinforced expectations of sustained high interest rates, while the Bank of Japan signaled a potential rate hike this month.
